# Phase 10 Federation Treaty Plan

Phase: 10.0.34 - Constitutional Multi-Civilization Coordination, Federation & Governance Treaty Doctrine  
Status: Planning-only. Runtime remains disabled.

## Objective

Define how multiple constitutional systems coordinate, federate, negotiate treaties, exchange trust, preserve sovereignty, and survive interdependence without centralization, fragmentation, domination, or hidden execution authority.

## Scope

- Constitutional federation doctrine.
- Inter-civilization coordination framework.
- Governance treaty standards.
- Sovereignty-preserving cooperation.
- Federated legitimacy continuity.
- Multi-entity constitutional coordination.
- Treaty survivability.
- Inter-system trust boundaries.
- Decentralized cooperation.
- Constitutional alliance survivability.
- Coordinated autonomy.
- Anti-imperial governance safeguards.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- APIs, providers, adapters, credentials, or OAuth.
- Prisma, Supabase, Twilio, email, or background jobs.
- Autonomous federation systems.
- Automatic treaty execution systems.
- Centralized governance authority.
- Inter-system execution authority.
- Repository mutation systems.
- Rollback execution.
- Deployment authority.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Planning docs remain isolated under governance documentation paths.
- Federation preserves sovereignty, dissent, and auditability.
- Treaties define purpose, scope, authority limits, amendments, disputes, and exits.
- Trust boundaries remain scoped, evidence-based, and reviewable.
- Coordination remains fail-closed under ambiguity.
- Runtime and deployment authority remain prohibited.
